item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
She looked after the spiders and now the spiders look after her!

This is the perfect mix of creepy and sweet. I love skips like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This sounds like a prequel to something like 1006. I'd imagine that when she passes the spiders would honor her memory with gardening instead of advocating for communism.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh! Dat last line! DX

*all the upvotes*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this one. Neat idea, nice execution.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SPIDERS FOR THE SPIDER GODDESS

All in all a great example of Heartwarming in a Scip, and makes me proud to upvote a such a literary work of art

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really enjoyed this, but the addendum at the end didn't really restate much. It just seemed to drag the article on longer than it needs to be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The idea is strange and in my opinion weak. But, I really like the self repairing effect. Close to a neutral vote.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is inarguably my new favorite SCP and i think i'm gonna draw it. Is there any way i can show you the drawing after i do?

Man, ever since +100 fell off the first page of Top Rated Pages, people have kinda stopped celebrating this, also I meant to delete this post because there's like a dozen that need this and I don't wanna set precedent but I accidentally posted it instead lol

anyways congrats

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I hate spiders, I shun the spider goddess. Death to all of her spawn foreverrrrrrr.

+1

(imagine if Telepathic Spider was full of tinier Irish grandmas)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What college did you attend, Arkham University?!?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"This Grandma is Full of Spiders"

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is 1 that I enjoyed. I was expecting an image of a salticid (I don't have arachnophobia, but Jumping spider faces - or anything that could be easily mistaken for such - creep me out) accompanying it, with the SCP itself being the "spider mother goddess" manifested as the jumping spider in the expected photo. The fact that it was sad & not creepy made me feel good, since you don't always see arachnid SCPs that aren't creepy. Hats off to you, writer.

Additionally, it (& the Foundation in general) was made even more enjoyable for me due to my personal real-world experience with stuff that would warrant SCP Foundation attention if the SCP Foundation existed in real life, including the fact that I dated a jorogumo in college.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As a guy who love spider, I really like this.

When I saw the title, I though it will be creepy and dangerous. I actually feel GREAT that it isn't.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's like a cat lady!

….. Except with spiders, and the spiders actually help her instead of giving free brain parasites like regular cats do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting, but I really expected some kind of a zinger at the end. This way, it just seems bland. It reminds me too much of SCP-204 instead of posing an interesting difference.

item.title: Re: Spiderbros
item.content:encoded:
I need something like it for my crockoaches problem.

Regardless, I enjoyed it a lot.

item.title: Re: Spiderbros
item.content:encoded: I swear Zyn, it's like you're a 4koma character.

item.title: Spiderbros
item.content:encoded:
At one point my room had a spider tenant. He lived on the outside of my windowsill, and sometimes when I was sitting at my desk he'd crawl out and say hello and scare me. I called him Spider-kun and he ate mosquitoes and ants and kept them out my room.

This article makes me happy. +1 for helpful spiders

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Should be fine. Fry got infested with worms that not only boosted his natural regeneration, but also made him smarter, stronger, etc.

It was actually a plot device used to tease the Fry/Leela relationship. Good episode.

Anyway, aside from "helpful colony of bugs living in someone," the two are very different. Wasn't thinking of Futurama when I read this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a relic of an older time: Dr. Mackenzie's Personnel File

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If this had just been a lady filled with spiders, I would have been disappointed (hah! curse this site for making me say things like that), but that they actively help keep her healthy really puts this into upvote range.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked this very much. It was touching also that she had begun to suffer from Alzheimers. I like to think of her a benevolent witch with spider familiars who is now suffering from the ravages of old ages.

I like that the spiders are caring for her =D
+1

Do you not have an author page, Aelanna? I'm trying to find a way to search for other articles by you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm a huge arachnphobe (phob?), but I did quite enjoy this article. That episode of Futurama was the first thing that popped into my head when I read it, but it didn't ruin my enjoyment at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is true, but it takes a lot of words to say it, many of which aren't that relevant. I think it could be summarized more succinctly via findings from many interviews.

And further, her unwillingness/inability to talk to the Foundation about it seems really, really contrived.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the interview shows something really important: She loves the spiders, and that's why they love her back.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Moreover, I saw them as being not angels but animal nanobots, of a sort. Repairing her beneficially, probably with an extradimensional connection to, I dunno, her house? where they replenish and take duty shifts. They're clearly alive, so it's not quite literally an angelic phenomenon, and I didn't get any sense of the religious from the article. It's good, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry about that, I'm quite new to the site. Thanks anyway

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The bit about the spiders living in interdimensional space didn't really go anywhere. Why can't they just live inside the old woman's body - helping aged internal organs to keep working, or replacing them entirely.

The latter would be a bit like one idea in David Cronenberg's film Shivers (aka They Came from Within). A parasite that takes over the function of a faulty organ.

Well, avoiding that cliche was part of the point. They're not parasites and they're not trying to take over. They're more like little spidery angels, going forth to do the mother goddess's bidding.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty good. I wouldn't doubt if she was far older than 87.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the skip, but the interview doesn't seem to add much. It's just a neighbour who's got no information to give - beyond their own mild puzzlement that some would care for spiders.

The bit about the spiders living in interdimensional space didn't really go anywhere. Why can't they just live inside the old woman's body - helping aged internal organs to keep working, or replacing them entirely.

The latter would be a bit like one idea in David Cronenberg's film Shivers (aka They Came from Within). A parasite that takes over the function of a faulty organ.

Overall, it's like a benign version of the plot of Phase IV.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is very out of place. You can copy the blackboxes by copying them from the How to Write an SCP guide. You can also ask questions about writing SCPs in the main forums or on IRC.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This may be out of place, but does anyone know how to insert those black boxes to hide information on the articles and stuff. I would really appreciate a reply, thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This skip was inspired by a real person, the uncle of a friend of mine who owned a plot of land in Florida where he raised banana spiders (the giant golden silk orb-weaver variety, not the brown ones that kill banana farmers in South America).

Edit: Dexanote informed me that there is an episode of Futurama with a roughly analogous plot in which Fry gains regeneration through super-smart worms or something. This was not intended to be similar to that in any way, but rather a story of a nice old lady who care for spiders, and one day the spider mother goddess decided to repay her kindness.
